message:
feat(fcp-core): add approval_tokens field to InvokeRequest

Per FCP Specification Section 7.4, InvokeRequest now carries approval tokens
for authorizing elevation, declassification, and execution-scope operations.

Changes:
- Add approval_tokens: Vec<ApprovalToken> to InvokeRequest struct
- Add ApprovalToken to protocol.rs imports
- Update doc comments to document the new field
- Fix test_handshake mut requirement in anthropic connector

This completes a key integration point for bead 1n78.8 (Provenance/Taint).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Goal
Validate ZoneCheckpoint/frontier advancement and fork detection semantics with deterministic unit/integration tests.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for checkpoint management and fork detection.

## Test Categories

### Checkpoint Advancement
- [ ] Normal advancement with quorum signatures
- [ ] Advancement blocked without quorum
- [ ] Checkpoint sequence monotonically increases
- [ ] Checkpoint binds AuditHead + RevocationHead
- [ ] Checkpoint signature verification

### Fork Detection
- [ ] Conflicting checkpoints detected
- [ ] Fork evidence is preserved
- [ ] Fork triggers alert/pause
- [ ] Fork resolution protocol
- [ ] No silent fork acceptance

### Convergence
- [ ] Nodes converge on same checkpoint
- [ ] Late-joining node catches up
- [ ] Partition recovery reaches consistency
- [ ] Checkpoint gossip propagation
- [ ] Convergence metrics tracking

### Frontier Management
- [ ] Frontier tracks latest valid checkpoint per zone
- [ ] Stale frontier detection
- [ ] Frontier update atomic with heads

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/checkpoint/`
- `checkpoint_valid.cbor`
- `checkpoint_fork_evidence.cbor`
- `convergence_scenario.json`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_fork_detection",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "zone_id": "z:work",
  "checkpoint_seq": 42,
  "fork_detected": true,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fork detection is reliable
- Convergence is achieved after partition heal
- All checkpoint operations are audit-logged

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-abwt","title":"TEST: Canonical Serialization Unit Tests (Deterministic CBOR, Schema Hash, CDDL Validation)","description":"# TEST: Canonical Serialization Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that serialization is deterministic and schema-bound.

## Test Categories

### Deterministic CBOR Encoding
- [ ] Same logical object → same bytes (always)
- [ ] Map key ordering is canonical (sorted)
- [ ] Integer encoding is minimal
- [ ] Float encoding is canonical
- [ ] No duplicate map keys
- [ ] Definite length encoding used

### Schema Hash Binding
- [ ] SchemaId (namespace, name, version) hashes correctly
- [ ] SchemaHash is 32 bytes (BLAKE3)
- [ ] Payload format: schema_hash || canonical_cbor_bytes
- [ ] Schema hash mismatch detected on decode

### Schema Validation
- [ ] Valid object passes schema check
- [ ] Missing required field rejected
- [ ] Wrong field type rejected
- [ ] Extra fields handled per policy (reject or ignore)
- [ ] Nested object validation works

### CDDL Compliance
- [ ] All FCP2 objects have CDDL definitions
- [ ] Generated bytes match CDDL constraints
- [ ] CDDL can be used for cross-language validation

### Roundtrip Tests
- [ ] Encode → decode → encode produces identical bytes
- [ ] All core object types roundtrip correctly
- [ ] Large objects roundtrip correctly
- [ ] Nested objects roundtrip correctly

### Bounds and Limits
- [ ] Oversized objects rejected at encode time
- [ ] Deeply nested objects bounded
- [ ] String length limits enforced
- [ ] Array length limits enforced

### Decode Safety
- [ ] Malformed CBOR rejected without panic
- [ ] Truncated input rejected
- [ ] Oversized length prefix rejected (DoS prevention)
- [ ] Recursive structure depth bounded

## Golden Vectors
- Each core object type with canonical bytes
- Schema hash computation examples
- Malformed inputs that must be rejected

## Property Tests
- Determinism: same input → same output
- Commutativity with schema: schema_hash(decode(encode(x))) == schema_hash(x)

## Fuzz Targets
- CBOR decode with arbitrary bytes
- Schema validation with malformed objects

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "object_type": "...",
  "schema_id": "...",
  "operation": "encode|decode|validate",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All core types have golden vectors
- Determinism verified by property tests
- Fuzz targets find no panics

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-aho","title":"fcp.s3: AWS S3 Compatible Object Storage Connector","description":"# fcp.s3 - AWS S3 Compatible Object Storage Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant S3-compatible object storage connector (AWS S3 and compatible endpoints) for bucket/object operations with strict NetworkConstraints and safe credential injection.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 81/100** — Important Tier 3 connector (Infrastructure/Storage family).

**Why Critical**: S3 is the **de facto standard for object storage**, used by virtually every cloud application. S3-compatible APIs are offered by AWS, GCP, MinIO, Cloudflare R2, etc. Essential for agents to read/write files, manage assets, and handle data storage.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (Infrastructure + Universal)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Object Operations
- Upload/download objects
- List objects in buckets
- Delete objects
- Generate presigned URLs

### Metadata Management
- Read object metadata
- Set custom metadata
- Content type handling
- Cache control

### Bucket Operations
- List accessible buckets
- Get bucket configuration
- Check permissions

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Upload this file to S3"
"Download the latest backup"
"List all files in the assets bucket"
"Generate a shareable link for this file"
"Archive old logs to cold storage"
"Sync this folder to S3"
"What's in the exports bucket?"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### ALL File-Based Connectors
**Universal file storage:**
- Store exports from any connector
- Central asset repository
- Backup destination

### Google Drive/OneDrive Integration
- S3 as backend storage
- Cross-cloud file sync
- Enterprise file management

### AI Provider Integration
- Store model artifacts
- Dataset management
- Training data access

### Terraform/Kubernetes Integration
- Infrastructure state storage
- Container image registry backing
- Configuration storage

### Agent Mail Integration
- Large attachment storage
- File sharing links
- Document archival

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### Provider Compatibility
S3-compatible APIs:
| Provider | Endpoint |
|----------|----------|
| AWS S3 | s3.{region}.amazonaws.com |
| MinIO | Custom endpoint |
| Cloudflare R2 | {account}.r2.cloudflarestorage.com |
| GCS (interop) | storage.googleapis.com |
| DigitalOcean Spaces | {region}.digitaloceanspaces.com |

### Recommended Crates
- `aws-sdk-s3` — Official AWS SDK
- `rusoto_s3` — Alternative SDK
- `reqwest` — For custom implementations

### Authentication
- AWS Signature V4
- IAM credentials
- STS temporary credentials
- Service account keys

### Storage Classes
| Class | Use Case |
|-------|----------|
| STANDARD | Frequently accessed |
| INTELLIGENT_TIERING | Variable access |
| GLACIER | Archive |
| DEEP_ARCHIVE | Long-term archive |

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:storage:s3:full` | Full access | All operations |
| `z:storage:s3:readonly` | Read-only | Download and list |
| `z:storage:s3:bucket:<name>` | Bucket-scoped | Single bucket |
| `z:storage:s3:write` | Write-only | Upload only |

### Data Sensitivity
- S3 often contains sensitive data
- Bucket policies critical
- Encryption requirements vary

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — REST API operations

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Varies by provider:
- AWS: `s3.*.amazonaws.com:443`
- Custom: Configured endpoint

### Credential Injection
- AWS credentials via `CredentialId`
- Access key ID + Secret
- STS tokens for temporary access
- Never write credentials to disk

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `s3.objects.read` — Download objects
- `s3.objects.list` — List objects
- `s3.objects.metadata` — Get metadata
- `s3.buckets.list` — List buckets

### Write Operations
- `s3.objects.write` — Upload objects
- `s3.objects.copy` — Copy objects

### Administrative (Dangerous)
- `s3.objects.delete` — Delete objects
- `s3.buckets.manage` — Bucket operations

### Presigned URLs
- `s3.presigned.get` — Generate download URLs
- `s3.presigned.put` — Generate upload URLs

---

## Operations (Representative)

### List Objects
```rust
let resp = client.list_objects_v2()
    .bucket("my-bucket")
    .prefix("exports/")
    .max_keys(100)
    .send()
    .await?;
```

### Upload Object
```rust
client.put_object()
    .bucket("my-bucket")
    .key("path/to/file.txt")
    .body(ByteStream::from_path("local/file.txt").await?)
    .content_type("text/plain")
    .send()
    .await?;
```

### Download Object
```rust
let resp = client.get_object()
    .bucket("my-bucket")
    .key("path/to/file.txt")
    .send()
    .await?;
let body = resp.body.collect().await?;
```

### Generate Presigned URL
```rust
let presigned = client.get_object()
    .bucket("my-bucket")
    .key("path/to/file.txt")
    .presigned(PresigningConfig::expires_in(Duration::from_secs(3600))?)
    .await?;
```

### Delete Object
```rust
client.delete_object()
    .bucket("my-bucket")
    .key("path/to/file.txt")
    .send()
    .await?;
```

---

## Multi-Provider Support

### Configuration Pattern
```rust
struct S3Config {
    provider: S3Provider,  // AWS, MinIO, R2, etc.
    endpoint: Option<String>,
    region: String,
    bucket: String,
    path_style: bool,  // true for MinIO
}
```

### Provider-Specific Notes
- **MinIO**: Requires path-style URLs
- **R2**: No bucket creation API
- **GCS**: Limited S3 compatibility
- **Spaces**: Full S3 compatibility

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Request signing
- Response parsing
- URL generation
- Provider configuration

### Integration Tests
- LocalStack or MinIO for simulation
- Multi-part upload testing
- Large file handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Bucket-level access control
- Audit logging for all operations

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real S3 calls in unit tests
- Upload/download works
- List operations functional
- Presigned URLs work
- Multi-provider support
- Large file handling (multipart)

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ep21.3","title":"TEST: fcp.asana Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-epqh","title":"FCP Connector Portfolio: Strategic Prioritization Framework","description":"# FCP Connector Portfolio: Strategic Prioritization Framework

## Goal
Define and maintain a rigorous prioritization framework for the connector portfolio (value, risk, effort, dependencies) so roadmap decisions are coherent, explainable, and user-focused.

## Executive Summary

This document captures the strategic thinking, prioritization rationale, and implementation roadmap for the Flywheel Connector Platform (FCP) connector portfolio. The goal is to maximize agent autonomy and capability while building a sustainable, high-quality connector ecosystem.

---

## Strategic Vision

### The Flywheel Effect
Connectors create a flywheel of value:
1. **More connectors** → Agents can do more things
2. **More capable agents** → More users adopt the platform
3. **More users** → More demand for connectors
4. **More demand** → Justification for building more connectors

### Design Principles
- **Quality over quantity**: Each connector should be production-ready, not a proof-of-concept
- **Ecosystem coherence**: Connectors should work together, enabling cross-service workflows
- **Security by default**: Default-deny, capability-based permissions throughout
- **Future-proof**: FCP2 spec compliance ensures consistency and upgradeability

---

## Prioritization Framework

### Value Assessment Model
Each connector is scored on five dimensions:

| Dimension | Weight | Meaning |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| **User Reach** | 25% | How many users need this service |
| **Agent Autonomy** | 25% | How much more can agents do with this |
| **Flywheel Synergy** | 20% | Integration potential with other connectors |
| **Strategic Position** | 15% | Competitive differentiation |
| **Implementation Cost** | 15% | Effort vs. payoff ratio |

### Tier Definitions

#### Tier 1: Critical Infrastructure (Score 93-100)
**Build immediately. Foundation for everything else.**

These connectors either:
- Enable the core agent workflow loop
- Provide capabilities no other connector can substitute
- Have massive user reach (>50% of target users)

Connectors:
- `fcp.twitter` (98) — Real-time social layer, high visibility
- `fcp.linear` (97) — Modern issue tracking, Beads sync
- `fcp.stripe` (96) — Payments/billing, revenue operations
- `fcp.youtube` (95) — Video/transcript access, research
- `fcp.browser` (95) — Universal fallback, any website
- `fcp.plaid` (94) — Banking/financial data
- `fcp.anthropic` (94) — Core AI provider
- `fcp.openai` (94) — Broadest AI capabilities
- `fcp.google-ai` (94) — Massive context, multimodal
- `fcp.vectordb` (93) — Memory/search infrastructure

#### Tier 2: High-Value Productivity (Score 83-92)
**Build in phase 2. Major capability expansion.**

These connectors:
- Serve large user segments
- Enable important workflow categories
- Have strong synergies with Tier 1

Connectors:
- `fcp.microsoft365` (91) — Enterprise productivity suite
- `fcp.jira` (89) — Enterprise issue tracking
- `fcp.twilio` (88) — Communications infrastructure
- `fcp.hubspot` (87) — CRM/marketing automation
- `fcp.airtable` (86) — No-code database
- `fcp.figma` (85) — Design collaboration
- `fcp.sentry` (85) — Error tracking/debugging
- `fcp.datadog` (84) — Observability platform
- `fcp.grafana` (83) — Open-source observability

#### Tier 3: Specialized Value (Score 70-82)
**Build in phase 3. Domain-specific capabilities.**

These connectors:
- Serve specific user segments very well
- Enable specialized workflows
- May be safety-critical (credentials, infrastructure)

Connectors:
- `fcp.kubernetes` (82) — Container orchestration
- `fcp.s3` (81) — Object storage
- `fcp.terraform` (80) — Infrastructure as code
- `fcp.1password` (79) — Credential management
- `fcp.arxiv` (78) — Research papers
- `fcp.zendesk` (77) — Customer support
- `fcp.semanticscholar` (77) — Citation analysis
- `fcp.annas-archive` (76) — Book/document search
- `fcp.reddit` (76) — Community discussions
- `fcp.docusign` (75) — E-signatures
- `fcp.homeassistant` (74) — Home automation
- `fcp.spotify` (72) — Music/podcast

#### Meta-Connectors: Force Multipliers
**Build throughout. Infrastructure for the infrastructure.**

These aren't service connectors but platform capabilities:
- `fcp.webhook-receiver` (88) — Inbound event gateway
- `fcp.cron` (86) — Scheduled task runner
- `fcp.mcp-bridge` — MCP server passthrough

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Synergy Map

```
                    Agent Mail (coordination hub)
                           ↑
         ┌─────────────────┼─────────────────┐
         ↓                 ↓                 ↓
    AI Providers     Data Sources      Communication
    (Anthropic,      (GitHub,          (Slack, Twilio,
     OpenAI,          Linear,           Discord,
     Google AI)       Stripe)           Email)
         ↓                 ↓                 ↓
         └─────────────────┼─────────────────┘
                           ↓
    ┌──────────────────────┼──────────────────────┐
    ↓                      ↓                      ↓
 Vector DB            CASS/CM                   BV
 (memory)          (search/memory)       (task tracking)
```

### Key Integration Patterns

#### 1. Development Workflow
```
GitHub PR → Linear issue → Agent investigates →
  → Sentry errors + Datadog metrics →
  → Agent proposes fix → PR merged → Linear updated
```

#### 2. Customer Success Workflow
```
Zendesk ticket → Agent analyzes →
  → Searches knowledge base → Drafts response →
  → If complex: creates Linear issue →
  → Updates HubSpot contact record
```

#### 3. Revenue Operations Workflow
```
Stripe payment event → Agent correlates →
  → Updates HubSpot deal → Sends Twilio notification →
  → If failed: creates Zendesk ticket →
  → Generates report in Airtable
```

#### 4. Research Workflow
```
User question → Agent searches ArXiv + Semantic Scholar →
  → Downloads papers → AI summarizes →
  → Stores in Vector DB → Returns synthesis
```

---

## Implementation Roadmap

### Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-4)
**Focus: Core agent capabilities**

- AI Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google AI
- Meta: Webhook Receiver, Cron
- Critical: Twitter, Linear, GitHub
- Storage: Vector DB, S3

### Phase 2: Productivity (Weeks 5-8)
**Focus: Business workflows**

- Enterprise: Microsoft 365, Jira
- Communication: Twilio, Slack
- CRM: HubSpot
- Observability: Sentry, Datadog, Grafana
- No-code: Airtable
- Design: Figma
- Payments: Stripe, Plaid

### Phase 3: Specialization (Weeks 9-12)
**Focus: Domain expertise**

- Infrastructure: Kubernetes, Terraform
- Research: ArXiv, Semantic Scholar, Anna's Archive
- Support: Zendesk
- Legal: DocuSign
- Security: 1Password

### Phase 4: Expansion (Weeks 13+)
**Focus: Long-tail value**

- Social: Reddit
- Media: Spotify, YouTube
- Home: Home Assistant
- Additional based on demand

---

## Quality Standards

### Every Connector Must:
1. **Pass FCP2 compliance runner** — Static and dynamic checks
2. **Have no real API calls in tests** — Mock-only unit tests
3. **Implement proper zones** — Capability-based security
4. **Support audit logging** — All operations tracked
5. **Handle rate limits** — Respect service quotas
6. **Document synergies** — Integration patterns explicit

### Bead Standards
Each connector bead must include:
- Strategic value score with justification
- Concrete agent use cases (not abstract)
- Flywheel synergies with specific connectors
- Zone architecture recommendations
- Capability model (read/write/admin)
- Representative operations with examples
- Testing approach
- Acceptance criteria

---

## Risk Considerations

### API Stability
- Services may change APIs
- Version pinning where possible
- Deprecation monitoring needed

### Rate Limits
- Different per service
- Budget enforcement at zone level
- Queue management for bursts

### Security
- Credentials never written to disk
- 1Password as credential source
- Audit trails mandatory

### Cost
- Some APIs charge per call
- AI providers charge per token
- Budget controls essential

---

## Success Metrics

### Coverage Metrics
- % of target workflows enabled
- # of connectors at each tier
- Cross-connector workflow coverage

### Quality Metrics
- FCP2 compliance rate
- Test coverage per connector
- API error rates

### Usage Metrics
- Operations per connector
- Workflow completion rates
- User satisfaction scores

---

## Appendix: Connector Index by Category

### AI Providers
- fcp.anthropic, fcp.openai, fcp.google-ai

### Communication
- fcp.slack, fcp.discord, fcp.telegram, fcp.twilio

### Development
- fcp.github, fcp.linear, fcp.jira

### Observability
- fcp.sentry, fcp.datadog, fcp.grafana

### Productivity
- fcp.microsoft365, fcp.notion, fcp.airtable

### CRM/Sales
- fcp.hubspot, fcp.stripe

### Financial
- fcp.plaid, fcp.stripe

### Research
- fcp.arxiv, fcp.semanticscholar, fcp.annas-archive

### Infrastructure
- fcp.kubernetes, fcp.terraform, fcp.s3

### Social
- fcp.twitter, fcp.reddit, fcp.youtube

### Support
- fcp.zendesk, fcp.intercom

### Security
- fcp.1password, fcp.bitwarden

### Design
- fcp.figma

### Legal
- fcp.docusign, fcp.pandadoc

### Personal
- fcp.spotify, fcp.homeassistant

### Meta-Connectors
- fcp.webhook-receiver, fcp.cron, fcp.browser

## Success Criteria
- A stable scoring model exists (value, risk, effort, dependencies) and is applied consistently across the connector list.
- Tiering/prioritization outputs are actionable: they produce concrete connector epic work with dependencies that align to the FCP2 platform build order.
- The framework can be updated incrementally without losing rationale (future maintainers can understand why rankings changed).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-eqb","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Event subscription and real-time updates","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Event subscription and real-time updates (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide streaming access to Home Assistant’s event bus via WebSocket:
- state_changed
- call_service
- custom events

## Scope
- Establish websocket session and authenticate.
- Subscribe to events with filters (domain/entity allowlists).
- Deliver events as FCP2 event envelopes with:
  - stable event IDs
  - correlation IDs

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.events.subscribe` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned (WS + HTTPS to HA host only).
- Streaming must be bounded/backpressured:
  - cap queue depth
  - drop/deny with explicit reason codes when overloaded
- Taint:
  - event payloads are tainted external input

## Deliverables
- Operation schema:
  - `ha.events.subscribe`
- Event envelope types:
  - `ha.event.state_changed`
  - `ha.event.call_service`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - reconnect + resubscribe
  - bounds/backpressure behavior
  - taint propagation

## Acceptance criteria
- Event streaming is reliable under reconnects and never causes unbounded memory growth.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f3xi","title":"TEST: Offline Capability Unit Tests (Availability Tracking, Pre-staging, Coverage Metrics)","description":"# TEST: Offline Capability Unit Tests

## Goal
Comprehensive unit tests for offline capability tracking from V2 spec section 21.

## Test Categories

### OfflineAccess Tests
- can_access() returns true when local_symbols >= k
- can_access() returns false when local_symbols < k
- coverage() calculates correct ratio (local_symbols / k)
- Edge cases: k=0, local_symbols=0, overflow

### OfflineCapability Tests
- Accessible map tracks multiple objects correctly
- Object removal updates capability state
- Query by zone returns correct subset
- Memory efficient for large object counts

### Pre-staging Tests
- Access pattern tracking updates correctly
- Frequent objects prioritized for pre-staging
- Pre-staging respects device storage limits
- Stale access patterns decay appropriately
- Pre-staging triggers before predicted access

### Integration with Stores
- Symbol store updates reflect in OfflineAccess

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f50","title":"fcp.reddit: User Posting and Commenting","description":"# fcp.reddit: User Posting and Commenting (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable controlled Reddit participation:
- submit posts
- submit comments
- edit/delete own content

These are real-world side effects and must be tightly capability scoped.

## Scope
- Submit post to a subreddit (text/link).
- Submit comment.
- Edit content.
- Delete content.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.post.write` (dangerous)
- `reddit.comment.write` (dangerous)
- `reddit.content.edit` (dangerous)

Policy guidance:
- Posting should often require ApprovalToken depending on zone policy.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Content safety:
  - outbound content must be treated as a “publication” action
  - receipts + audit events include subreddit + post/comment id
  - never log post bodies

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.post.submit`
  - `reddit.comment.submit`
  - `reddit.content.edit`
  - `reddit.content.delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - request formation
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission
  - error mapping (subreddit rules, forbidden)

## Acceptance criteria
- Write operations are mechanically authorized, explainable, and auditable.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-uek4","title":"TEST: Provenance/Taint Unit Tests (Label Propagation, Approval Enforcement, Sanitizer Receipts)","description":"# TEST: Provenance/Taint Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that information flow controls work correctly and cannot be bypassed.

## Test Categories

### Provenance Field Handling
- [ ] origin_zone correctly tracked
- [ ] current_zone updated on zone crossing
- [ ] integrity_label correctly propagated
- [ ] confidentiality_label correctly propagated
- [ ] taints accumulated correctly

### Taint Flags
- [ ] PUBLIC_INPUT flag applied to public data
- [ ] EXTERNAL_INPUT flag applied to external API responses
- [ ] UNVERIFIED_LINK flag applied to unverified references
- [ ] USER_SUPPLIED flag applied to user inputs
- [ ] PROMPT_SURFACE flag applied to data reaching prompts
- [ ] Flags accumulate (union) correctly

### Label Merge Rules
- [ ] integrity = MIN(input_integrities) - verified
- [ ] confidentiality = MAX(input_confidentialities) - verified
- [ ] Property tests: merge is associative and commutative
- [ ] Edge cases: empty inputs, single input, many inputs

### Integrity Flow (Biba Model)
- [ ] Down-flow (high→low integrity): allowed without approval
- [ ] Up-flow (low→high integrity): requires Elevation approval
- [ ] Missing elevation approval → rejection with reason_code
- [ ] Valid elevation approval → allow

### Confidentiality Flow (Bell-LaPadula Model)
- [ ] Up-flow (low→high confidentiality): allowed without approval
- [ ] Down-flow (high→low confidentiality): requires Declassification approval
- [ ] Missing declassification approval → rejection
- [ ] Valid declassification approval → allow

### ApprovalToken Verification
- [ ] scope = Elevation: validates integrity transition
- [ ] scope = Declassification: validates confidentiality transition
- [ ] from_label/to_label binding enforced
- [ ] Time validity (iat/exp) enforced
- [ ] Signature verification succeeds/fails correctly

### SanitizerReceipt Verification
- [ ] Receipt covers input object IDs
- [ ] Receipt from authorized sanitizer
- [ ] Receipt within validity period
- [ ] Taint flags correctly reduced after sanitization
- [ ] Partial sanitization (some taints remain) handled

### Cross-Zone Operations
- [ ] Zone crossing requires appropriate approvals
- [ ] Labels adjusted correctly on zone entry
- [ ] DecisionReceipt captures zone crossing denial

### Attack Scenarios (Adversarial)
- [ ] Taint laundering: attempt to remove taint without receipt → rejected
- [ ] Elevation bypass: low-integrity data to high-integrity zone without approval → rejected
- [ ] Declassification leak: high-confidentiality data to low-confidentiality sink → rejected
- [ ] Forged sanitizer receipt: invalid signature → rejected
- [ ] Stale approval: expired ApprovalToken → rejected

## Golden Vectors
- Provenance merge scenarios with expected output
- ApprovalToken for elevation and declassification
- SanitizerReceipt format and verification

## Property Tests
- Merge rule properties (associative, commutative)
- No taint reduction without valid receipt
- Labels never increase integrity / decrease confidentiality without approval

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "flow_type": "integrity|confidentiality",
  "from_label": "...",
  "to_label": "...",
  "approval_present": true,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All label propagation paths tested
- Approval enforcement verified
- No taint bypass possible

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ug8j","title":"fcp.trello: Trello Kanban Board Connector","description":"# fcp.trello Connector


## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant Trello connector for boards/cards automation with strict capabilities and webhook ingestion.

Flywheel Connector Protocol implementation for Trello - the popular kanban board platform for project management and team collaboration.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in the connector’s manifest/cap map bead if present).
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)

- `operational`
- `streaming`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Request/Response + Webhook

## Overview

- **Value Score**: 63/100 (Tier 4)
- **Archetype**: Request-Response + Webhook
- **Platform**: Trello REST API
- **Model**: Simple kanban (boards, lists, cards)

## Operations

### Board Management
- Create, read, update, delete boards
- Board settings and preferences
- Board backgrounds and power-ups
- Board templates

### List Management
- Create, read, update, archive lists
- List positioning and ordering
- Move lists between boards

### Card Operations (CRUD)
- Create, read, update, delete cards
- Card positioning within lists
- Move cards between lists/boards
- Card due dates and reminders
- Card cover images

### Checklists
- Create, read, update, delete checklists
- Checklist items management
- Item completion tracking
- Copy checklists between cards

### Labels
- Create, read, update, delete labels
- Assign/remove labels from cards
- Custom label colors and names

### Attachments
- Upload attachments to cards
- Link external URLs
- Delete attachments
- Attachment previews

### Members
- List board members
- Add/remove members from boards
- Assign/unassign members to cards
- Member permissions

## Zone Architecture

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.


### Zone: board_read
- Read board details and settings
- List boards for user
- Read lists and list positions
- Read cards and card details
- Read checklists and items
- Read labels and attachments
- Read member information

### Zone: card_write
- Create/update/delete cards
- Manage card positions
- Create/update/delete checklists
- Manage checklist items
- Add/remove labels from cards
- Upload/delete attachments
- Assign/unassign members to cards

### Zone: board_admin
- Create/update/delete boards
- Create/update/delete lists
- Create/update/delete labels
- Add/remove board members
- Manage board settings
- Archive/unarchive boards

## Dependencies

- `fcp-core`: Core FCP types and traits
- `fcp-oauth`: OAuth 1.0a authentication (Trello uses OAuth 1.0a)
- `fcp-webhook`: Real-time webhook handling for board/card events
- `fcp-ratelimit`: Rate limiting (Trello: 100 requests/10s per token, 300/10s per key)
- `fcp-telemetry`: Metrics and tracing

## Technical Details

### Authentication
- OAuth 1.0a flow
- API Key + Token authentication
- Token expiration handling

### Rate Limits
- 100 requests per 10 seconds per token
- 300 requests per 10 seconds per API key
- Rate limit headers: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`

### Webhook Events
- Card created/updated/deleted
- Card moved between lists
- Checklist item completed
- Member added/removed
- Board updated
- List created/archived

### API Considerations
- Nested resources (board > list > card > checklist)
- Batch operations support
- Field selection for response optimization
- Pagination via `limit` and `before`/`since` parameters

## Implementation Plan

1. **Phase 1**: Core board and list operations
2. **Phase 2**: Card CRUD and positioning
3. **Phase 3**: Checklists and labels
4. **Phase 4**: Attachments and members
5. **Phase 5**: Webhook integration

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Full board CRUD operations
- [ ] Complete list management
- [ ] Card operations with positioning
- [ ] Checklist and label support
- [ ] Attachment upload/management
- [ ] Member management
- [ ] Webhook event handling
- [ ] Rate limit compliance
- [ ] OAuth 1.0a authentication flow
- [ ] Zone-based permission model
- [ ] Comprehensive test coverage

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ug8j.1","title":"fcp.trello: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"## Goal
Produce the full FCP2 manifest and capability map for this connector.

This bead is where we translate the connector’s functional surface (operations) into a **mechanically checkable** contract:
- operation schemas + schema IDs
- capability families per operation
- per-operation NetworkConstraints (default deny)
- sandbox profile requirements
- declared state model (stateless / singleton_writer / crdt)

## Required content (must be explicit)
- Operation list is exhaustive and stable for the current phase.
- Capability IDs DO NOT encode hostnames/ports.
- NetworkConstraints are minimal and per-operation.
- Risk tier for each operation (Safe / Dangerous).
- Explicit bounds: timeouts, max response sizes, pagination limits.

## Tests
- Add good/bad manifest examples for the compliance runner.

## Acceptance Criteria
